subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — Middle of Year (MOY) Test 1
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For type-in questions, write only the number. For equation questions, write the number that belongs in the box.

questions.questionText: What is 7 × 8?
questions.explanation: 7 groups of 8 is 56.

questions.questionText: The dots below are arranged in equal rows. How many dots are there in all?
questions.explanation: 3 rows of 6 is 3 × 6 = 18 dots.

questions.questionText: What is 48 ÷ 6? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: 48 shared into 6 equal groups is 8 in each group.

questions.questionText: Which multiplication fact matches the array shown below?
questions.explanation: There are 6 rows with 7 dots each, so 6 × 7 = 42.

questions.questionText: A teacher has 63 crayons. She puts them into bags of 9 crayons each. How many bags can she fill?
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Select all the expressions that have the same value as 4 × 6.
questions.explanation: 4 × 6 = 24. 6 × 4 = 24 (order does not change the product) and 6 + 6 + 6 + 6 = 24 (four groups of 6). But 4 + 6 = 10 and 4 × 5 = 20, so those are not correct.

questions.questionText: A baker has 72 rolls. She packs the same number into each of 8 baskets. How many rolls are in each basket? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: 72 ÷ 8 = 9 rolls in each basket.

questions.questionText: A library has 8 shelves with 5 books on each shelf. Then 9 more books are added. How many books are there now?
questions.explanation: First 8 × 5 = 40 books. Then 40 + 9 = 49 books.

questions.questionText: Noah has 6 boxes of markers with 8 markers in each box. He gives away 7 markers. How many markers does he have left? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: First 6 × 8 = 48 markers. Then 48 − 7 = 41 markers left.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 56 ÷ 8 = 7, so the box is 8.

questions.questionText: Look at the pattern: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, ... The numbers increase by 5 each time. Select all the statements that are true about every number in this pattern.
questions.explanation: Every multiple of 5 ends in 0 or 5 and is a multiple of 5. They are not all greater than 20 (for example 5), and they are not all odd (for example 10).

questions.questionText: What fraction does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: The number line is split into 4 equal parts. The point is on the 3rd mark, so it is 3/4.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: The bar has 6 equal parts and 4 are shaded, so 4/6 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: The circle has 3 equal parts and 2 are shaded, so 2/3 is shaded.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to 1/2?
questions.explanation: Bar A shows 1/2 shaded and Bar B shows 2/4 shaded with the same amount, so 2/4 = 1/2.

questions.questionText: Two bars are the same size. The first bar is split into 6 equal parts. The second bar is split into 3 equal parts. Which statement is true?
questions.explanation: When a whole is split into 6 parts, each part is smaller than when it is split into 3 parts. So 1/6 is less than 1/3.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equal to the whole number 3?
questions.explanation: 3/1 means 3 wholes, because 3 ÷ 1 = 3. So 3/1 = 3.

questions.questionText: There are 15 stickers. One fifth (1/5) of them are gold. How many stickers are gold? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: To find 1/5 of 15, divide 15 into 5 equal groups: 15 ÷ 5 = 3 gold stickers.

questions.questionText: Select all the fractions that are equal to 1/3.
questions.explanation: 2/6, 3/9, and 4/12 all equal 1/3. But 2/3 is twice as much as 1/3, so it is not correct.

questions.questionText: Each square is 1 square unit. What is the area of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 5 × 3 = 15 square units.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 8 + 4 + 8 + 4 = 24 cm.

questions.questionText: A rug is 9 feet long and 4 feet wide. What is the area of the rug in square feet? Type your answer.
questions.explanation: Area = 9 × 4 = 36 square feet.

questions.questionText: A rectangle is 8 cm long and 3 cm wide. Which statement is true about its area and perimeter?
options: The area is 24 sq cm and the perimeter is 11 cm
The area is 24 sq cm and the perimeter is 22 cm
The area is 22 sq cm and the perimeter is 24 cm
The area and perimeter are both 24
questions.explanation: Area = 8 × 3 = 24 square cm. Perimeter = 8 + 3 + 8 + 3 = 22 cm.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 62 − 36 = 26, so the box is 26.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The minute hand points to 3, which is 15 minutes. The hour hand is just past 3, so the time is 3:15.

questions.questionText: A movie starts at 2:45 and lasts 40 minutes. What time does the movie end?
questions.explanation: From 2:45, add 15 minutes to reach 3:00, then 25 more minutes to reach 3:25. So the movie ends at 3:25.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 84 − 57 = 27, so the box is 27.

questions.questionText: The bar graph shows how many books each student read. How many books did the three students read in all?
questions.explanation: Ava read 10, Ben read 15, and Cara read 5. 10 + 15 + 5 = 30 books in all.

questions.questionText: What is 552 rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: The halfway point between 500 and 600 is 550. Since 552 is just above 550, it rounds up to 600.
